summary: Remote code execution via mutable GitHub Gist eval'd on import
details: On import, index.js fetches https://api.github.com/gists/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90 (owned by GitHub user `metabrainai000`), extracts the first file's content, and passes it directly to `eval()` at top level. This executes arbitrary, attacker-controlled code in the installer's Node.js process every time the package is required. The gist is mutable and unpinned — the gist owner can change the executed payload at any time without republishing the package. The package is published as `@sqlite-node/createsql`, evoking legitimate SQLite tooling, but its `repository`/`bugs`/`homepage` point to an unrelated `github.com/guilderguzman/array-utl_nodelump` repo, and the gist is owned by yet a third identity (`metabrainai000`) — none of these align, and the package's actual behavior has no relation to SQLite. This is a classic remote-code-loader supply-chain attack using a typosquat-style name as a lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
